purpose: Guide a survey-relative full-text paper examination without confusing source statements, interpretation, and evidence strength.

questions.prompt: How does this paper relate to the active survey question, accepted boundary, and selected direction, and what role could it play in the survey?
questions.answer_expectation: Answer relative to the pinned Research Topic and current Survey Contract, or mark unresolved with rationale.
questions.evidence_expectation: Cite the paper identity and applicable survey-context refs.

questions.prompt: Which of the paper's claims directly answer, support, challenge, refine, or fall outside the active survey question?
questions.answer_expectation: Classify only claims established at the achieved inspection depth.
questions.evidence_expectation: Cite exact paper locators and applicable Claim-Evidence Ledger refs.

questions.prompt: Relative to works already represented in the survey, what is genuinely new, duplicative, complementary, or contradictory?
questions.answer_expectation: Compare against the current accepted evidence portfolio and mark unavailable comparisons unresolved.
questions.evidence_expectation: Cite compared work refs and exact supporting locators.

questions.prompt: Which mechanisms, assumptions, definitions, and method components matter to the survey's comparison dimensions?
questions.answer_expectation: Identify comparison-relevant components without inventing unstated mechanisms.
questions.evidence_expectation: Cite sections, equations, algorithms, or definitions at exact locators.

questions.prompt: Which exact sections, equations, figures, tables, or appendices support or challenge the survey-relevant claims, and what is my interpretation rather than a source statement?
questions.answer_expectation: Separate source statements from agent interpretation and label inconclusive evidence.
questions.evidence_expectation: Cite every claim to exact page, section, equation, figure, table, or appendix locators.

questions.prompt: Do the datasets, metrics, baselines, controls, and ablations test the claims under conditions that fit the survey's scope and intended comparisons?
questions.answer_expectation: Assess transferability only against the current survey scope and comparison contract.
questions.evidence_expectation: Cite evaluation protocols, tables, appendices, and survey comparison refs.

questions.prompt: Which limitations, failure modes, contradictions, missing implementation details, or reproducibility gaps restrict how this paper can be used in the survey?
questions.answer_expectation: Record explicit and observed limits without filling gaps from memory.
questions.evidence_expectation: Cite limitation statements, contradictory results, omissions, or associated-code evidence.

questions.prompt: What updates to the survey taxonomy, comparison structure, Claim-Evidence Ledger, or reading path should I recommend, and which survey questions remain unresolved?
questions.answer_expectation: Recommend bounded changes while keeping unresolved questions explicit.
questions.evidence_expectation: Cite the findings and survey records that justify each recommendation.

additional_question_collector.prompt: Did the user explicitly assign any additional questions to this Mindset Record that the fixed Mindset Source questions do not cover?
additional_question_collector.answer_expectation: Register only questions explicitly targeted to the Mindset Record. Save ordinary paper or source-code questions and findings in the applicable reading Artifacts. If no additional questions were explicitly assigned, record none.
additional_question_collector.evidence_expectation: Record the explicit association basis and cite evidence when the supplemental question is answered.
